channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Iran Declares Continued Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Iran’s newly appointed Supreme Leader, Mojtaba Khamenei, has stated that the country will continue to use the Strait of Hormuz as leverage against the United States and Israel. The comments, delivered...

item.title: Safa Proposes Major Cuts to Bafana Bafana and Banyana Banyana Tournament Bonuses
item.description: The South African Football Association (Safa) has proposed a new bonus structure for the national teams, Bafana Bafana and Banyana Banyana, which could see players receive significantly lower earnings...

item.title: Natalia Ladha Excels in Both Swimming and Science Studies Abroad
item.description: Natalia Ladha, a well-known Tanzanian swimmer, has made a name for herself not only in sports but also in academics. After years of breaking national records and competing internationally, including a...

item.title: FG Commits to Strengthening Collaboration with UNDP to Boost Reform Programs
item.description: The Federal Government don show serious interest to waka closer with the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) to speed up President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s reform programmes. Hon. Femi Gbajabiamila...

item.title: Toronto police seize SUV linked to shooting near U.S. consulate
item.description: Toronto police have recovered an SUV reportedly connected to a shooting incident that occurred outside the U.S. consulate in Toronto. Authorities have not confirmed the identities of the individuals i...

item.title: Driver Stops Thieves After Woman’s Bag is Snatched in Daylight
item.description: In Santiago, CCTV footage showed two thugs attempting to snatch a woman’s sling bag in broad daylight. The lady, dressed in a yellow T-shirt and black trousers, was walking alone when the passenger of...

item.title: PM Modi outlines measures to strengthen India's energy security amid global fuel crisis
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has emphasised that his government is taking all necessary measures to safeguard India's energy security, especially in light of the ongoing conflict between the US and Ir...

item.title: Former Village Chairman Acquitted as Court Finds Insufficient Evidence in Murder Case
item.description: The High Court of Tanzania, Manyara Circuit, has cleared Mr John Mulki, former chairman of Berme A sub-village in Babati District, of charges in connection to the death of Petro Julian. The alleged in...

item.title: UN Security Council debates Iran nuclear program amid US, Russia, and China tensions
item.description: On March 12, 2026, tensions flared at the United Nations as the US and its Western allies confronted Russia and China over Iran’s nuclear program. The dispute occurred during a Security Council sessio...

item.title: UK asylum appeal backlog nearly doubles, reaching over 80,000 cases
item.description: The backlog of asylum appeals in the United Kingdom has reached an unprecedented level, with 80,333 cases pending at the end of 2025, nearly double the 41,987 recorded a year earlier. The average wait...

item.title: Tanzania to streamline regulatory agencies to boost business and investment
item.description: Dodoma. The Tanzanian government announced a review of public agencies and institutions that regulate business activities, aiming to reduce bureaucratic red tape and excessive levies that make it hard...

item.title: Many Americans Make Financial Sacrifices to Afford Health Care
item.description: A recent study from the West Health-Gallup Center on Healthcare in America shows that millions of Americans are forced to make difficult trade-offs to afford health care. More than 82 million people, ...

item.title: Middle East conflict triggers unprecedented disruption to global oil supply
item.description: Oil markets are facing a historic disruption as the ongoing war in Iran blocks tankers from transporting millions of barrels of crude each day. The International Energy Agency (IEA) has described the ...

item.title: Ultra Lotto 6/58 Jackpot Reaches Nearly P100 Million After No Winner
item.description: The Ultra Lotto 6/58 jackpot continues to grow as it nears the P100-million mark, according to the Philippine Charity Sweepstakes Office (PCSO). The latest draw, held on Tuesday, produced the winning ...

item.title: Seventy Years of Africa-China Partnership: Opportunities and Challenges Ahead
item.description: As Africa and China mark 70 years of diplomatic relations, the partnership is entering a new chapter with significant economic and social implications. China remains Africa's largest trading partner a...

item.title: CRTC eliminates extra fees for modifying or cancelling internet and cellphone plans
item.description: Canada's Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission (CRTC) has announced that telecom companies will no longer be allowed to charge fees for changing, cancelling, or activating internet and ce...

item.title: Jaipur man allegedly kills wife and daughter, remains with their bodies until police arrive
item.description: In a tragic incident in Jaipur, a 40-year-old man, Surjeet Singh, allegedly killed his wife Saroj Devi (35) and daughter Anshika (8) on Wednesday night. According to the police, the man reportedly att...

item.title: Easy Hoisin Chilli Beef Stir-Fry Recipe by Adam Liaw
item.description: Adam Liaw presents a quick and flavour-packed stir-fry recipe featuring hoisin chilli beef. Perfect for a midweek dinner, this dish combines thinly sliced topside beef marinated with cornstarch, sesam...

item.title: Eswatini takes in four additional third-country deportees from the United States
item.description: The government of Eswatini confirmed on Thursday that it had received four more third-country nationals deported from the United States, raising the total number of deportees received to at least 19. ...

item.title: Choosing Ideology Over Prosperity Keeps People Poor
item.description: This article talks about how people dey prefer ideology pass practical results even if e mean say citizens go suffer. Writer Charles Makakala dey use countries like South Korea, Japan, and Taiwan as e...
